Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.animation

Class FlixelAnimateRig

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.animation.FlixelAnimateRig
public final class FlixelAnimateRig extends Object
Immutable, pre-resolved multi-part rig for an Adobe Animate texture-atlas character. Built by FlixelAnimateRigLoader and consumed by FlixelAnimateSprite. The same rig structure represents both nested symbol timelines (Better Texture Atlas style) and flat document timelines that place ASI instances directly on the stage timeline.

Data shape

The rig is a dictionary of named clips (for example "Idle", "Left"). Each clip holds an array of keyframes, one per visible frame of the animation. Each keyframe holds an ordered array of parts, which are the individual bitmap slices that must be drawn in back-to-front order to produce the final composited image for that moment in time.

Each FlixelAnimateRig.Part stores:

  • An integer index into atlas selecting which bitmap slice to draw.
  • A fully baked Affine2 that already contains (a) the Flash MX or M3D matrix chain from the root symbol or stage down to the leaf bitmap, (b) a Y-axis flip that converts Adobe Animate's Y-down bitmap space into libGDX's Y-up texture-region space, and (c) an anchor translation that shifts the whole rig so the anchor-clip bounding box starts at (0, 0).

Because every matrix is baked at load time, the per-frame draw path is reduced to one translate, one scale, and one affine concatenation per visible bitmap.

Anchor space

The anchor clip defines the bounding box used to size the sprite's hitbox: parts are pre-shifted so the union of their rectangles on anchor-clip frame 0 fits exactly inside (0, 0) to (anchorWidth, anchorHeight). All other clips are baked in the same coordinate space, so switching clips does not move the rig relative to the sprite's hitbox.

Merging multiple atlases

A rig can grow at runtime by merging additional Adobe Animate exports through FlixelAnimateRigLoader.append(FlixelAnimateSprite, FlixelAnimationController, String, String, String). Appended atlases share the original rig's anchor space (preserved on the rig as anchorMinX / anchorMinY / anchorHeight), so a character's body stays visually pinned to the same world position when game code switches between, say, an "idle" atlas and a separate "miss" atlas. Frames from the appended atlas are appended to atlas and the appended clip names are added to clips, with later entries overwriting earlier ones on name collisions.

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static final class 
    FlixelAnimateRig.Clip
    A named animation clip, for example "Idle" or "Left Miss".
    static final class 
    FlixelAnimateRig.Keyframe
    A single visible frame of a clip.
    static final class 
    FlixelAnimateRig.Part
    A single bitmap draw call inside a FlixelAnimateRig.Keyframe.

    Name of the clip whose frame-zero bounding box defines the rig's anchor-space rectangle.

    Height in pixels of the anchor-clip bounding box.

  • Field Details

    • atlas

      @NotNull public final @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Array<FlixelFrame> atlas
      The shared atlas regions indexed by FlixelAnimateRig.Part.atlasIndex. This is the same list installed on the owning FlixelSprite through FlixelSprite.applySparrowAtlas(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.graphics.FlixelGraphic, com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Array<me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.graphics.FlixelFrame>), so callers may look up a region either way.

    • clips

      @NotNull public final @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.utils.ObjectMap<String,FlixelAnimateRig.Clip> clips
      Map of clip name (for example "Idle") to its baked FlixelAnimateRig.Clip.

    • anchorClipName

      @NotNull public final @NotNull String anchorClipName
      Name of the clip whose frame-zero bounding box defines the rig's anchor-space rectangle. Usually the string "Idle" if present, otherwise the first clip from the loaded data (for example the default clip synthesized when an export has no label layer).

    • anchorWidth

      public final float anchorWidth
      Width in pixels of the anchor-clip bounding box. Used to size the owning sprite's hitbox.

    • anchorHeight

      public final float anchorHeight
      Height in pixels of the anchor-clip bounding box. Used to size the owning sprite's hitbox.

  • Method Details

    • getClip

      @Nullable public @Nullable FlixelAnimateRig.Clip getClip(@NotNull @NotNull String name)
      Looks up a clip by its label-layer name (for example "Idle", "Left Miss").
      Parameters:
      name - The clip name, matching the "N" field on the label layer of Animation.json. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The FlixelAnimateRig.Clip, or null if no clip with that name exists in this rig.

    • getClips

      @NotNull public @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.utils.ObjectMap<String,FlixelAnimateRig.Clip> getClips()
      Returns the live map of clip name to FlixelAnimateRig.Clip. Callers must treat the returned map as read-only; mutating it invalidates the rig.
      Returns:
      The internal clip map. Never null.
